## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the headline on your webinar registration page. A good headline is key to your registration success. Of course, there's lots of things that are key to your registration success, but a good headline, it's right up there. So let's talk about your headline. Purposes of a headline. First one is to get them to read the rest of the webinar description, to pique their interest. You want them thinking, hey, I need this. And it begins to describe what's in the webinar to dangle the hook for your webinar in front of them. So we've talked about the hook, how important it is to have one. Here's the headline dangles that hook in front of them. The description and the button sets that hook. So a good headline, it's simple, not trying to communicate multiple concepts. It draws people in with a question, a statement that invites consideration, maybe controversy, maybe a promise, basketball. You've all seen this concept of an alley-oop, where someone's coming down, they throw the ball up right next to the rim so that someone can jump up there and dunk it in. That's what the headline is. The headline is the alley-oop that sets it up for your description and your bullet points about what they're going to discover in the webinar, to dunk that thing right down in. Now, your headline and the description must be consistent with the type of content that you'll be teaching. So you saw this earlier in another module, that there are five different kinds of content you can teach in a webinar, the step-by-step content, the in-depth coverage, the tips content, objection handling, or a system overview. Your headline needs to be consistent with that. And so everything flows through from the very first communication, the headline, through the description, through everything that happens in your sequence to get people to actually attend the webinar and the webinar itself. And so this headline needs to set that process up at the beginning. A great headline that doesn't accurately describe your webinar is actually a bad headline. Now, there's also a concept around headlines called pre-imposed heads, meaning pre-imposed headlines. A pre-head is text that goes before the headline, usually to give it context or to say who should be paying attention to it or to ask a question. So something like, are you struggling to build your Instagram following? A post-head is the text that goes after the headline to deepen the communication or to make it apply to them. So maybe
